titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Koethe Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 5, 2020 at 05:41

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 28 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 29 (mold damaged photographs): ‡a Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 30 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Ownership & Copyright
userestrict.p: The John Koethe Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2011.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: John Koethe Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: John Koethe
bioghist.p: John Koethe (1945- ) is an American poet, essayist, and retired professor of philosophy at the University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, notebooks, clippings, writings of others, photographs, awards, student papers, teaching papers, audiovisual materials, and digital media documenting the life and work of poet John Koethe. Correspondence includes letters from other poets and writers including John Ashbery, Robert Dash, John Godfrey, Mary Kinzie, David Lehman, James Schuyler, Susan Stewart, and others. Writings include drafts of books by Koethe, individual poems, and other writings. Writings of others include manuscripts by John Ashbery, John Godfrey, and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eleven series: I. Correspondence, circa 1960s-2009. II. Writings, 1960s-2011. III. Notebooks, 1966-1969. IV. Clippings and printed material, 1965-2010. V. Writings of others, 1949-2010. VI. Photographs, 1970s-2000s. VII. Awards and memorabilia, 1959-1963. VIII. Student and teaching papers, 1967-2000s. IX. Other papers, 1960s-2000s. X. Audiovisual material, 1987-2008, undated. XI. Electronic media, 2005, undated.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Correspondence is arranged alphabetically, with miscellaneous unsorted correspondence at the end.

did.unittitle: Misc. - Responses to Late Wisconsin Spring

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Writings are organized into three subseries: Books, Individual poems, and Other writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Your Day", "Sodden", "Copley Square", "Mission Bay", "Summer", "Power and Persuasion", "Below the Coast", "Mary Astor's Journal", "Men and Wives", "Indications", "A Sunday Drive", "Belgium", "Personal Life", partial draft of a book compilation called Boy's Life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Narrow Way"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems written by Susan Koethe, Instructions for taking care of the plants while she (Susan) was away on a trip.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "By the Mystic's Statue"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Parody of Wallace Stevens' "Thirteen Ways of Looking at a Blackbird"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most poems from "The Late Wisconsin Spring" with the exception of "Anacoluthia".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Some", "Punchinellos and Dancing Dogs", based a drawing in the Fogg Art Museum at Harvard, letter to Jimmy (Schuyler?).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: unpublished poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes for an unwritten poem.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early version of "Domes", discarded poems, travelogue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems droped from the final version of "Blue Vents" early version of "Commerce" titled "Trade", "Pomona MCMLXVII" parody of Pond's "Canto LXXXI".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early version of "Mistral", "The Little Boy", "A Long Lesson", xeroxes of John Ashbery's poems, Xerox of Marjorie Perloff peice on Frank O'Hara, poems by Tohmas Disch; Notes, posters and public materials for a poetry reading and philosophy lecture organized by David Lehman at Hamilton College 1979; David Lehman's discussion guidlines for a panal at the 1977 MLA on John Ashbury resulting in a collection of essays titled "Beyond Amazment" edited by Lehman, Keothe's contribution to this discussion to panel "The Metaphysical Subject" is included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of a book manuscript submitted (unsuddessfully) to the Frank O'Hara Award competition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Domes" with holographic revisions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Song", letter from James Atlas regarding his anthology "Ten Americans", list of poems read at New College 1971 or 1972, copies of poems written 1967-1969, poems by John Godfrey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Tiny Figures in Snow", "Seascape with Clouds and Birds" written after a trip to Florida; letter regarding a poetry reading at New York University with Joe Ceravolo, Clark Coolidge, arranged by Kenneth Koch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems by John Godfrey(?) typed on Kothe's typewriter some may be collaborations between Godfrey and Koethe but not from "Images of France" which they collaborated on in 1968 and spent most of their time "smoking dope and writing" but sometime after that.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Skiing poems by John Godfrey(?), other poems by Koethe his senior year at Princeton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes for an unfinished poem Milwaukee, 1973; unpublished poem written at the Roten Gallery in Cambridge, 1968; poem dedicated to Koethe and his wife by John Godfrey(?); typscripts and notes for various poems "song" (here called "The Sleepyhead's Song"), "Natural Knowledge", "The Hand in the Breast Pocket"; holographic draft of reveiw of six books for "Poetry", typscript of reveiw, letter from Daryl Hine; untitled poem on Roten Gallery stationary, poem typscripts pre-"Domes", philosophy notes, "notes and messing around poems", 1968-1969; fellowship application, prose fantasy, collaborarion with John Godfrey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems from Koethe's junior year at Princeton University, includes "Alice" and other unpublished poems; mimeos of poems distributed to William Meredith's poetry class; letter to Tom Clark submitting poems to "Paris Review" where he was editior, none of the poems were accepted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various poems, Notes and typescript of "They", fragment of a play "The Paper Clip Relief", manuscripts for "Process" and "Your Day", early draft of "Your Day" titled "Heavy Fires", drafts of "In the Middle", "Alice", "Solo", a large number of typescripts written by Koethe when he was "heavily influenced by the Black Mountain Poets, especially Robert Duncan includes "Burning Water" and " The Nassua Lit."; Letter from Alan Downer telling Koethe his poem had won the Academy of American Poets Prize.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Koethe's earliest existing manuscripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Constists of notebooks, mostly dating from Koethe's undergraduate years at Princeton University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of clippings and printed materials containing works by and about John Koethe. Also includes information about Koethe, including copes of his curriculum vitae, and other biographical materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of print articles, Milwaukee Poet Laurate articles

did.unittitle: John Koethe - Published in "The Nassau Lit"
John Koethe - Published in "The Nassau Lit"
John Koethe - Published in "A Poetic Celebration of the Hudson River"
John Koethe - Published in "Poetry Everywhere", DVD (Restricted)
John Koethe - Published in "United Artists Fourteen"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Writings of others are organized alphabetically by author, with unidentified writings filed at the end.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings of others include manuscripts by John Ashbery, John Godfrey, and others.

did.unittitle: Ashbery, John - Copy from "Furioso", Friar Lawrence's cell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Sunrise in Suburbia", "The Double Dream of Spring" and others

did.unittitle: Ashbery, John - manuscript - "Description of a Masue"
Ashbery, John - manuscript - "The New Spirit" , with ALS
Ashbery, John - manuscript-"The Recital" and "The System"
Ashbery, John - manuscript-"Self-Portrait in a Conves Mirror"
Cole, Henri - manuscript - "A Deviant Alphabet for My Goddaughter", with ALS
Cole, Henri - manuscript - ""Street of Repentance: A Provençal Daybook", with ALS
Corbett, William and Robert Nunelly - manuscript - "Fire Exit"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed also signed and numbered (20), Bouwerie Editions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Poem for Kings, Independence Day Eve and others

did.unittitle: Godfrey, John - manuscript - "Twenty-Six Poems", with ALS
Godfry, John and John Koethe - manuscript - "Images of France"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence

did.unittitle: Assorted poetry by others and poetry related material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newsletter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 14 issues including number 1.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four prints by Grace Hartigan, Felix Pasilin(?), Alfred Leslie, and Gandy Brodie.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Photographs are arranged in the order in which they were received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8X10 inch black and white photographs taken by Koethe of street scenes, family life, and portraits of Robert Dash, John Ashbery and philosopher Roger Albritton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of various kinds and sizes, including snapshots of John Godfrey, Marjorie Welish, Douglas Crase and Mary Kinzie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11x13 photograph of Koethe with untitled typescript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8x10 black and white photographs with various ephemera, publications and typescript

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material, due to mold damange. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clipping "Running back John Riggins, without his tie, was led from the party by Jim Seymore, left, and hal Wingo", Newspaper clipping "Cooling System", photo album "Scenes from Berlin, Paris, New Jersey.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook related to Koethe's winning of the National Science Fair
National Science Fair photographs, descriptions and mathmatical formulas, awards and correspondence
"Quest: Select Writings from San Diego City Schools"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two issues, each with writings by John Koethe, "Ronald", and "The Hospital".

did.unittitle: Awards and citations presented to Koethe in junior high and highschool and college

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Student and teaching papers include Koethe's undergradute thesis and doctoral dissertation and documentation of Koethe's teaching at Northwestern University and elsewhere.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Intentionality and Synonymy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sceptical Arguments and Uncertainty

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Taught at Northwestern

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings, printed material, ephemera, artwork and personal papers.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Robert Thompson and John Koethe, 11/10/87, Intuflo Intro by Susan Baran, video by Vladimir Urban, unedited copy #1
